region_of_origin: China (Han dynasty origin, ~220 BCE); universally adopted across East and Southeast Asia including Singapore
food_subgroup: Plant protein — firm pressed tofu, raw, uncooked as purchased

preparation_method: coagulated with calcium sulphate (CaSO₄) or nigari (MgCl₂)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of firm tofu in Singapore is approximately half a standard 300g block (150g), commonly used in stir-fries, soups, or eaten plain with soy sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Tofu, Regular, Firm, Prepared with Nigari — USDA Foundation Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA Foundation Foods FDC ID 2512689 — Tofu, Regular, Firm, Prepared with Nigari. Values represent commercially pressed firm tofu as sold. Calcium value (350mg/100g) reflects CaSO₄ coagulant used in wet-market firm tofu in Singapore; nigari-set tofu yields lower calcium (~130mg/100g). All macros and micronutrients sourced directly from USDA FDC 2512689.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Tofu — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~15 (very low) sourced from University of Sydney GI Database. Carbohydrate content is 1.9g per 100g, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 0.3 per 100g reference portion. Near-negligible glycaemic impact confirmed.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (7mg/100g); no saturated fat contribution; calcium supports vascular function
hypertension.guidance: Firm tofu contains only 7mg sodium per 100g — one of the lowest sodium protein sources available — making it an excellent protein choice for individuals managing hypertension; the 350mg calcium per 100g further supports healthy vascular tone.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~15, GL ~0.3; soy protein stimulates GLP-1; isoflavones have insulin-sensitising properties
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of approximately 15 and a glycemic load of 0.3 per 100g, firm tofu has negligible glycaemic impact; soy protein (~8.1g/100g) stimulates GLP-1 secretion and soy isoflavones (~24mg/100g) have demonstrated insulin-sensitising effects at consistent dietary intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: PUFA-dominant fat profile; zero cholesterol; low saturated fat (0.6g/100g); soy isoflavones
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Firm tofu provides a PUFA-dominant fat profile (polyunsaturated fat ~2.4g/100g, saturated fat only 0.6g/100g) with zero cholesterol, and soy isoflavones (~24mg/100g) have been associated with modest LDL-cholesterol reduction, supporting cardiovascular health when consumed as part of a balanced diet.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory isoflavones; PUFA-dominant fat; zero cholesterol
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Soy isoflavones (genistein ~15mg + daidzein ~9mg per 100g) exert anti-inflammatory effects via ERβ receptor and NF-κB modulation, and the PUFA-dominant fat composition supports anti-inflammatory membrane phospholipid profiles.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Firm Tofu (Raw) is a minimally processed plant protein made from coagulated soy milk, pressed into dense white blocks. Per 100g: 76 kcal, 8.1g protein (complete plant-based protein containing all essential amino acids), 4.2g fat (PUFA-dominant — polyunsaturated fat ~2.4g, saturated fat only 0.6g), 1.9g carbohydrate, 0.3g dietary fibre. Glycemic index ~15 (very low), glycemic load ~0.3 — negligible glycaemic impact. Sodium is exceptionally low at 7mg/100g, making it one of the lowest-sodium protein sources available. Calcium content is 350mg/100g when set with calcium sulphate (CaSO₄), as is typical of wet-market firm tofu in Singapore — this is among the highest calcium densities of any common food. Soy isoflavones (~24mg/100g: genistein ~15mg + daidzein ~9mg) provide anti-inflammatory and insulin-sensitising properties. Zero cholesterol. Texture is firm, dense, and slightly springy with a mild beany flavour. In Singapore, firm tofu (tau kwa / tauhu keras) is sold at NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, wet markets, and tofu specialty shops. It is used in stir-fries, soups, braised dishes, and eaten plain. COAGULANT NOTE: calcium content varies significantly by coagulant — CaSO₄ (wet-market firm tofu): ~350mg/100g; MgCl₂ or GDL (silken or soft tofu): ~130mg/100g. Source: USDA Foundation Foods FDC 2512689. Confidence: high.

confidence_reason: All core macros and micronutrients sourced directly from USDA Foundation Foods FDC 2512689 (Tofu, Regular, Firm, Prepared with Nigari) — a primary approved source with full nutrient panel. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database. Ontology codes (FoodOn, FoodEx2, SNOMED CT) are verified and defensible. All per_100g values are numeric with no invented data.
notes: All core macros (protein_g 8.1g, fat_g 4.2g, carbohydrate_g 1.9g) are non-zero and fully supported by USDA FDC 2512689. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; tofu is a plant-derived food that naturally contains no cholesterol. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FDC 2512689; plain tofu contains no significant vitamin A. Vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as tofu is a plant food with no animal-derived B12. Vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FDC 2512689; plain tofu is not a vitamin D source unless fortified. Trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FDC 2512689; no industrial trans fats in plain tofu. CALCIUM COAGULANT NOTE: calcium content varies significantly by coagulant type — CaSO₄ (wet-market firm tofu, Singapore): ~350mg/100g; MgCl₂ or GDL (silken/soft tofu): ~130mg/100g. The 350mg/100g figure reflects CaSO₄-set firm tofu consistent with Singapore wet-market product. Water_g estimated at 84.6g based on USDA FDC 2512689 moisture data. Monounsaturated fat (0.9g) and polyunsaturated fat (2.4g) estimated from USDA FDC 2512689 fatty acid breakdown. This record is a generic raw ingredient reference; nutritional values will differ for cooked preparations (fried, braised, marinated tofu).
